response: The internet traces its roots to 1960s U.S.-funded networking research, with ARPANET’s first message sent in 1969 and a decisive shift to TCP/IP in 1983. Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 creation of the World Wide Web—HTML, URLs, HTTP—plus the 1993 Mosaic browser opened the network to the public, fueling a 1990s dot-com boom that crashed in 2001 yet left giants such as Amazon and Google. Social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube) in the mid-2000s and the iPhone-driven mobile web from 2007 onward, alongside 2006’s advent of cloud computing (AWS), further reshaped communication and commerce. Today the internet, now used by more than 5 billion people, underpins nearly every facet of global life.
